title: Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri
what_description: Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri is a botanical taxon within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its biennial or perennial habit and glabrous, yellow-green stems. Its leaves are lanceolate or ovate-lanceolate, with an acute or obtuse apex and dentate margins, measuring 2-10 cm in length. The plant produces small, yellow or white flowers in a racemose inflorescence, consisting of 4-petaled, 5-8 mm in diameter sepals and 4-petaled, 6-8 mm in diameter petals. The fruit is a silique, 2-6 cm in length, dehiscent and glabrous, containing 2-4 seeds. It is native to Central Asia and the Caucasus region.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri are inflammation, fever, and fungal infections, which it reduces through its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri has been shown to inhibit the influenza virus, making it effective against influenza, and has also demonstrated anti-rheumatic properties, alleviating arthritis. Its antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it a treatment for pneumonia, and its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties provide relief from eczema. Additionally, Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri has been found to inhibit the herpes simplex virus and has been used to treat herpes, as well as rheumatism and psoriasis. Its antiviral and antibacterial properties also make it effective against viral infections, urinary tract infections, asthma, diarrhea, skin infections, bronchitis, respiratory issues, bacterial infections, warts, and allergies.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri are its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, allowing it to inhibit the growth of various pathogens and prevent infections. Additionally, this plant exhibits antioxidant properties, thanks to the bioactive compounds like saponins and volatile oils that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Furthermore, Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri displays anti-inflammatory activity, which is mediated by the presence of flavonoids and saponins that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases. The immunomodulatory effects of this plant are also noteworthy, as it contains bioactive compounds like alkaloids that modulate the immune response and enhance its functionality. Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri has also been found to possess antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids that inhibit viral replication and prevent the spread of viral infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri are quercetin and kaempferol, two flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, kaempferol also exhibits anti-cancer activities by inhibiting cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis. Isatisin, a compound derived from isatin, is responsible for the plant's antimicrobial properties, exhibiting activity against various bacteria and fungi. Isatin itself has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential anti-cancer properties by inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. The flavonoids present in Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri also contribute to its overall antioxidant capacity, protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. These constituents work synergistically to provide the plant's therapeutic benefits.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri are tincture, infusion, isatis tea, decoction, and salve. Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri tincture is prepared by extracting the plant's root and aerial parts with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, commonly used in traditional medicine for its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. Isatis tea is prepared by infusing the dried root and aerial parts in hot water, traditionally used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and rheumatism. A decoction is also prepared from the root and aerial parts, simmered in water to extract the bioactive compounds, used to treat various conditions such as bronchitis and tonsillitis. A salve is prepared by infusing the plant's aerial parts in oil, traditionally used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri improperly include interacting with medications such as blood thinners, anticoagulants and immunosuppressants, which may increase the risk of bleeding or worsen existing medical conditions. Additionally, Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri may cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, as it contains isatin, a toxic compound that can irritate the stomach and intestines. Furthermore, improper use of Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri may trigger allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, in sensitive individuals. Long-term misuse of Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri has been linked to liver damage and increased liver enzymes, indicating potential liver toxicity. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Isatis cappadocica subsp. besseri to avoid these side effects and ensure safe use.
